active_items.title: EU AI Act — High-Risk AI System Classification
active_items.description: The EU AI Act classifies AI systems used in product management decision support as 'limited risk' with transparency obligations. However, autonomous decision-making features that influence hiring, credit, or access to services may be classified as 'high-risk' requiring conformity assessment, risk management, and human oversight.

product_impact.summary: ProductOS V10 must ensure autonomous features include mandatory human review gates for any decision that could affect access to services. Transparency labels must be visible on all AI-generated recommendations. High-risk classification triggers apply only if ProductOS is used for regulated decision domains.

active_items.title: EU Data Act — Data Sharing and Portability Requirements
active_items.description: The EU Data Act requires that users of connected products and related services have access to data generated by their use. For SaaS products, this means PM tooling data (research, decisions, analytics) must be exportable in machine-readable format upon user request.

product_impact.summary: ProductOS already supports workspace-level export. Need to document data portability compliance and add bulk export API for enterprise customers.

active_items.title: US Executive Order on AI — Safety and Security Standards
active_items.description: Executive Order 14110 requires developers of foundation model-based systems to report safety test results. NIST is developing AI Risk Management Framework standards. ProductOS uses LLM-based agents — safety testing and red-teaming documentation may be required for enterprise procurement.

product_impact.summary: Current scope: NIST standards are voluntary for non-foundational-model developers. Enterprise buyers may start requiring NIST AI RMF alignment. Monitor standard development and prepare compliance documentation proactively.

active_items.title: UK Data Protection and Digital Information Bill
active_items.description: The DPDI Bill proposes reforms to UK GDPR, including streamlined data protection requirements and reduced documentation burden for low-risk processing activities. May affect how ProductOS handles user data from UK-based PMs and customers.

product_impact.summary: Proposed changes are favorable (reduced burden). Monitor for final passage and adjust privacy documentation accordingly.

upcoming_deadlines.description: Limited-risk AI systems must implement transparency obligations: users must be informed they are interacting with AI, outputs must be labeled as AI-generated.
upcoming_deadlines.consequence_of_missing: Fines up to 7.5M EUR or 1.5% of global annual turnover. Loss of EU market access.

impact_summary.top_action_item: Implement AI transparency labels on all ProductOS V10 generated outputs before EU AI Act compliance deadline (August 2026)
